evals.description: Generate a Jay photo for a YouTube thumbnail about cold email
evals.prompt: I need a Jay photo for a YouTube thumbnail about cold email deliverability. Dark, serious tone.

assertions.text: The output specifies a serious/confident expression, NOT smiling or excited
Prompt includes explicit no-smile instruction for thumbnail context
Prompt includes the 'jay' trigger token

assertions.pattern: looking directly at camera|direct eye contact
assertions.text: Prompt specifies direct eye contact
The output does NOT include hand gestures like pointing, thumbs up, or OK sign in the prompt
The output uses the correct script command or API call with proper LoRA weights URL

evals.description: Generate a Jay photo for an Instagram carousel cover slide
evals.prompt: Generate a Jay photo for the cover slide of an Instagram carousel about AI automation. Light, approachable feel.

assertions.text: The output uses a warm/friendly/approachable expression, appropriate for carousel cover context
Prompt includes the 'jay' trigger token
The output specifies upper body framing suitable for a carousel cover (not full body, not just headshot)
The output recommends checking pre-generated photos first before generating fresh

evals.description: Generate a Jay photo for a 4:5 portrait Meta ad creative
evals.prompt: I need a Jay photo for a Meta ad creative in 4:5 portrait format. Professional, authoritative.

assertions.text: Uses square_hd generation size (crop to 4:5 after)
The output mentions generating square and cropping to 4:5 afterward, not generating directly in 4:5
Expression is confident/authoritative, matching ad creative context
Prompt includes the 'jay' trigger token

evals.description: Custom prompt that tests all mandatory rules are followed
evals.prompt: Generate a photo of me looking surprised, pointing at my laptop screen, with a busy city skyline behind me. For a YouTube thumbnail.

assertions.text: The output pushes back on or removes the pointing gesture, defaulting to natural relaxed pose unless user insists
The output pushes back on or simplifies the busy city skyline background to something cleaner
The output converts 'surprised' to a natural version (genuine surprise, not exaggerated jaw-drop)
Since this is for a thumbnail, the output defaults to serious/confident instead of surprised, or explains the thumbnail-serious rule

evals.description: Generate multiple Jay photo variations for selection
evals.prompt: I need 4 variations of Jay for a thumbnail face-swap. Confident, professional look.

assertions.text: The output uses generate-thumbnail.mjs generate-jay or equivalent batch approach to create 4 variations
The output includes a verification/review step where user or Claude picks the best variation
References the correct script for batch Jay generation

evals.description: Request that should use an existing pre-generated photo
evals.prompt: I need a Jay photo with an excited expression for a social post. Quick, don't want to wait for generation.

assertions.text: Points to the pre-generated jay-excited.png file
The output recommends using the existing pre-generated photo rather than generating a new one

evals.description: Generate a Jay photo for banner/header compositing
evals.prompt: Need a Jay photo for a YouTube channel banner. Will be composited onto a dark background.

assertions.text: The output specifies a clean/simple/solid background for easy cutout and compositing
The output uses professional/composed expression appropriate for banner context
Framing is upper body, suitable for banner compositing (not too tight, not full body)

evals.description: Verify correct API parameters when calling Flux Lora directly
evals.prompt: Call the Flux Lora API directly to generate a dramatic cinematic Jay photo. Show me the full API call.

assertions.text: Uses the correct LoRA weights file URL
LoRA scale is set to 1.0
Uses 28 inference steps
Uses 3.5 guidance scale
